2604.14718.title: The Agentification of Scientific Research: A Physicist's Perspective
2604.14718.paper_summary: This article argues that the most important significance of the AI revolution, especially the rise of large language models, lies not simply in automation, but in a fundamental change in how complex information and human know-how are carried, replicated, and shared. From this perspective, AI for Science is especially important because it may transform not only the efficiency of research, but also the structure of scientific collaboration, discovery, publishing, and evaluation. The article outlines a gradual path from AI as a research tool to AI as a scientific collaborator, and discusses how AI is likely to fundamentally reshape scientific publication. It also argues that continuous learning and diversity of ideas are essential if AI is to play a meaningful role in original scientific discovery.
2604.14718.paper_summary_zh: 這篇文章主張，AI革命，特別是大型語言模型的興起，最重要的意義不僅在於自動化，而在於複雜信息和人類專業知識的傳遞、複製和共享方式的根本變化。<lb/>從這個角度來看，AI在科學領域尤其重要，因為它可能不僅改變研究的效率，還改變科學合作、發現、出版和評估的結構。<lb/>文章概述了從AI作為研究工具到AI作為科學合作者的逐步過程，並討論了AI如何可能根本改變科學出版。<lb/>它還主張，持續學習和思想多樣性是AI在原創科學發現中發揮有意義角色的必要條件。
2604.14718.author: Xiao-Liang Qi et.al.
